understanding.goal_understanding: Navigate the level by moving the controllable piece(s) on the 3-pixels-per-cell grid to reach goal tiles (shown as '+' in the semantic grid), potentially using multiple selectable pieces and movable blocks to access or satisfy the goal condition within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction (x changes left/right, y changes up/down). If a pushable object (block) is directly adjacent in the movement direction, the move can push it along with the selected piece, provided nothing blocks the block’s destination. Walls and some boundaries can prevent movement, but the 'movement.*_blocked' indicator is only a hint—moves may still succeed or fail depending on the specific object/boundary behavior. Levels can contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with an exact pixel-coordinate position switches control to the selectable at that position, after which subsequent MOVE actions apply to the newly selected piece.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls, but the heuristic can be wrong; try moving right to test if any wall is passable or if movement has special behavior.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls, but the heuristic can be wrong; try moving right to test if any wall is passable or if movement has special behavior.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | All adjacent directions are walled in for the current piece; switch control to the other selectable to probe for a movable path.
history.reasoning: All adjacent directions are walled in for the current piece; switch control to the other selectable to probe for a movable path.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | All four directions are adjacent to wall; test if any direction still allows movement despite the heuristic by attempting right.
history.reasoning: All four directions are adjacent to wall; test if any direction still allows movement despite the heuristic by attempting right.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | Current selectable is boxed in by walls on all four sides; switch back to the other selectable to explore for a path toward the goal.
history.reasoning: Current selectable is boxed in by walls on all four sides; switch back to the other selectable to explore for a path toward the goal.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_UP | All adjacent tiles read as walls, but hints can be misleading; test an upward move to see if any wall behaves like a passable boundary or triggers a shift.
history.reasoning: All adjacent tiles read as walls, but hints can be misleading; test an upward move to see if any wall behaves like a passable boundary or triggers a shift.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_DOWN | All directions show adjacent walls but movement hints can be misleading; try moving down to see if the boundary allows sliding or exit to a new area.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls but movement hints can be misleading; try moving down to see if the boundary allows sliding or exit to a new area.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls but the hint isn’t definitive; probe MOVE_RIGHT to test if the wall blocks or allows some interaction/slide.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls but the hint isn’t definitive; probe MOVE_RIGHT to test if the wall blocks or allows some interaction/slide.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_UP | All directions look blocked by walls, so probe one direction to confirm whether the wall is truly impassable from here.
history.reasoning: All directions look blocked by walls, so probe one direction to confirm whether the wall is truly impassable from here.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re fully enclosed by walls on all four sides, so try moving into a wall to see if it behaves like a portal/phase boundary or triggers any interaction.
history.reasoning: We’re fully enclosed by walls on all four sides, so try moving into a wall to see if it behaves like a portal/phase boundary or triggers any interaction.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in by walls on three sides; try moving right into the adjacent selectable to see if it can be pushed or swaps/merges, potentially opening space toward the goal.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on three sides; try moving right into the adjacent selectable to see if it can be pushed or swaps/merges, potentially opening space toward the goal.

history.summary: Turn 11: CLICK | This selectable is completely enclosed by walls on all four sides, so switch control to the other selectable that may have access to the goal area.
history.reasoning: This selectable is completely enclosed by walls on all four sides, so switch control to the other selectable that may have access to the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent goal/wall tiles actually block movement; try moving into the right-side adjacent space.

history.summary: Turn 13: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear trapped by walls on all sides; test if the right wall boundary has any special behavior despite being marked blocked.
history.reasoning: We appear trapped by walls on all sides; test if the right wall boundary has any special behavior despite being marked blocked.

history.summary: Turn 14: MOVE_LEFT | We’re boxed in by walls on all sides; the only potentially passable adjacent tile is left where the goal overlaps, so try moving left onto/through the goal space.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all sides; the only potentially passable adjacent tile is left where the goal overlaps, so try moving left onto/through the goal space.

history.summary: Turn 15: MOVE_LEFT | We’re adjacent to the goal on the left; moving left should place this selectable onto the goal area if it’s passable.
history.reasoning: We’re adjacent to the goal on the left; moving left should place this selectable onto the goal area if it’s passable.
